danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile alien lifeforms appear on screen during Labyrinth exploration and Dungeon corridors
danger_signals.meaning: Player is in combat zone; life meter will decrease if hit

danger_signals.visual_form: Large alien creature at end of Dungeon corridor
danger_signals.meaning: Critical combat section; must defeat to progress and unlock safety device
Guardian has taken damage; if fully depleted, Guardian explodes and game ends

danger_signals.visual_form: Dungeon entry point requires specific action or key
danger_signals.meaning: Cannot access this safety device yet; need clues or progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Portal with specific symbol; no matching key in inventory
danger_signals.meaning: Area blocked; player lacks required key to progress through this territory

safety_signals.visual_form: Round, brightly colored blue creature in room
safety_signals.meaning: Checkpoint location; safe haven for dispensing advice, upgrades, passwords, and game resume point

safety_signals.visual_form: Accessible room within Labyrinth connected by warp panel
safety_signals.meaning: Safe trading location; can exchange power chips for weapon upgrades and capacity increases

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated Labyrinth room accessible via warp panel
safety_signals.meaning: Safe location to record progress; allows game resumption with retained progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectable items found in Labyrinth or dropped by defeated bosses
safety_signals.meaning: Restores life meter and provides resources; safe to collect

safety_signals.visual_form: Round, brightly colored red creature as item or NPC
safety_signals.meaning: Increases power chip capacity; provides strategic advantage and safety through resource abundance

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Warp panel symbols correspond to territorial areas controlled by different alien factions
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct alien lifeform designs differentiate tribes; enemies in each territory appear faction-specific
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Different Dungeon corridors and Labyrinth regions house different alien tribes; territorial segregation indicates faction control

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Gynoid transformer protagonist; aerobot capable of humanoid and spaceship forms
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player transforms between top-down humanoid explorer form (Labyrinth) and spaceship combat form (Dungeon)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Blue Landers serve as allied NPCs providing support; predecessor messages indicate prior Guardian attempts

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Messages left throughout Naju serve as faction markers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Message panels scattered in rooms reveal story; sole survivor's first message serves as introduction; later messages provide tactical hints

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game premise: alien world hurtling toward planet Earth, loaded with hostile lifeforms
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must infiltrate and deactivate ten safety devices to trigger self-destruct; urgency drives non-linear exploration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Five distinct alien tribes control different regions; Dungeon corridors and Labyrinth sections visually segregated by faction territory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different enemy types appear in different zones; player must navigate through hostile territories; combat intensity varies by region

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dungeon corridors destroyed and disappear after boss defeat; visual landscape changes as player completes objectives
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player returns to Labyrinth after each Dungeon completion; rewards (power-ups, keys) unlock new pathways; map grid updates reflect destroyed corridors

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guardian explodes when life meter depletes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game ends; player must restart or load saved password; no continuation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Locked warp panels with unmatched key symbols block visual pathways
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player cannot access certain territories; must explore alternate routes to find matching keys; non-linear gating mechanism

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Power chip depletion affects secondary weapon availability and shop purchase capability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must choose between weapon usage and upgrades; strategic resource management visible through UI

environmental_storytelling.example: Predecessor messages reveal tactical hints about sealed corridors and safety device locations; sole survivor's story advances narrative of failed prior attempts
Different Labyrinth regions and Dungeon corridors feature distinct visual designs corresponding to five alien tribes; player learns faction identity through environmental aesthetic
Warp panel symbols match corresponding keys; symbols serve as visual language communicating which territories are accessible and which are locked; guides non-linear exploration path
After defeating Dungeon boss, corridor visually collapses/disappears; return to Labyrinth shows changed landscape reflecting player progress; world reacts to player actions
Blue Lander positions throughout Labyrinth indicate safe zones and communication hubs; players learn to recognize these as strategic rest points and information sources
Shops accessible only via specific warp panels; serve as economic and narrative nodes where player can exchange resources and gain world knowledge

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Top-down perspective exploration; can navigate freely between screens; interact with NPCs, collect items, unlock warp panels via keys
Scrolling shooter perspective; auto-advances through corridor; focused on combat against corridor-specific enemies and boss; exit triggers return to Labyrinth
Attack Guardian on sight; deal damage to life meter; can be defeated to clear paths and obtain power-ups; behavior varies by tribal affiliation and location

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Positioned at end of Dungeon corridor
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Significantly more aggressive and resilient than standard enemies; require strategic combat; defeat triggers safety device deactivation and corridor destruction
Provides password generation; accepts power chip currency for upgrade trades; serves as checkpoint marker; dispenses advice and world context
When collected, increases maximum power chip capacity; strategic resource multiplier; provides player advantage
Visually present but inaccessible; block territorial access; create gating mechanism for non-linear exploration
Become accessible; transport player to new territories; reveal new Labyrinth sections or Dungeon corridor access; enable progression

notes: The Guardian Legend uses a dual-mode manifestation system: Labyrinth (exploration/top-down action-adventure) and Dungeon (scrolling shooter) visually and behaviorally distinct to communicate gameplay mode and world context. Alien tribal territorial control is communicated through environmental design, enemy variants, and warp panel access restrictions. The safety device activation mechanic creates visual world-state changes (corridor destruction) that reinforce player progression. Blue Landers serve as both mechanical checkpoints and narrative anchors, marking safe zones in a hostile world. The warp panel symbol-matching system uses visual communication to gate exploration and encourage non-linear pathfinding.
